content:
The complete Nyquist plot of the open-loop transfer function $G(s) H(s)$ of a feedback control system is shown in the figure.

If $G(s) H(s)$ has one zero in the right-half of the $s$-plane, the number of poles that the closed-loop system will have in the right-half of the $s$-plane is
explanation:
Given Nyquist plot of G(s)H(s) is shown below

Case 1 :

Here, $G(s) H(s)$ has one zero in the right half of s-plane i.e. $Z=1$.

04 According to principle of argument

$$ N=P-Z \text { (Anti clockwise direction) } $$ ........(i)

Where,

$N=$ Number of encirclements of Nyquist plot of $G(s) H(s)$ about origin in anti-clockwise direction

$P=$ Number of poles of $G(s) H(s)$ in right half of s-plane.

$Z=$ Number of zeros of $G(s) H(s)$ in right half of s-plane.

Here, $N=-2$ (anti clockwise direction)

$$ Z=1 $$

From equation (i)

$$ \begin{aligned} & -2=P-1 \\\\ & P=-1 ........(ii)\end{aligned} $$

So, poles cannot be negative in numbers so no more further discussion on this.

Case 2 : If we assume Nyquist contour in anti clockwise direction then according to principle of argument

$$ N=P-Z \text { (Clockwise direction) } $$ ..........(iii)

Where,

$N=$ Number of encirclements of Nyquist plot of $G(s) H(s)$ about origin in clockwise direction

$P=$ Number of poles of $G(s) H(s)$ in right half of s-plane.

$Z=$ Number of zeros of $G(s) H(s)$ in right half of s-plane.

Here, $N=2$ (Clockwise direction)

$$ Z=1 $$

From equation (iii)

$$ \begin{aligned} 2 & =P-1 \\\\ P & =3 .......(iv) \end{aligned}$$

According to Nyquist stability criteria

$$ N=P-Z \text { (Anti clockwise direction) } $$ .........(v)

Where, $N=$ Number of encirclements about $(-1+0 j)$ in clockwise direction

$Z=$ Number of closed loop poles in right half of s-plane.

$P=$ Number of open loop poles or poles of $G(s) H(s)$ in right half of s-plane.

From given Nyquist plot number of encirclements about $(-1+0 j)$ is

$$ N=1-1=0 $$

Using equation (v)

$$ \begin{aligned} & 0=P-Z \\ & Z=P \\ & Z=3 \end{aligned} $$

So here number of poles in right half of s-plane is 3 .

Hence, the correct option is (D).

content:
A unity feedback system that uses proportional - integral (PI) control is shown in the figure.

The stability of the overall system is controlled by tuning the PI control parameters $K_p$ and $K_I$ The maximum value of $K_I$ that can be chosen so as to keep the overall system stable or, in the worst case, marginally - stable (rounded off to three decimal places) is $\_\_\_\_$
explanation:
$$ \text { On Redrawing the mentioned figure we get, } $$

From above mentioned figure, we have

$$ \begin{aligned} G(s) & =\frac{K_p S+K_i}{S} \cdot \frac{2}{S^3+4 S^2+5 S+2} \\ & or,=\frac{2\left(K_p S+K_i\right)}{S^4+4 S^3+5 S^2+2 S} \\ H(S) & =1 \end{aligned} $$

Following is the Characteristic equation

$$ \begin{aligned} & 1+G(S) H(S)=0 \\ \text { Or we can say, }  & 1+\frac{2\left(K_p S+K_I\right)}{S^4+4 S^3+5 S^2+2 S}=0 \end{aligned} $$

Following is the other Characteristic equation

$$ S^4+4 S^3+5 S^2+\left[2 K_P+2\right] S+2 K_I=0 $$

Now on using the Routh Table

On having the Necessary Condition we can say that

On following Hurwitz Polynomial Condition all the coefficients of the characteristic equation should have same sign (Here > 0)

In comparison to the characteristic Equation

$$ \begin{array}{lrl} \text { We have } & 2 K_i>0 \\ & \Rightarrow K_i>0 \\ \text { or, } & 2 K_P+2>0 \\ \text { or, } & K_P>-1 \end{array} $$

On following the Sufficient Condition, we can say that Sufficient Condition

From referring to the above Routh table all the elements of First column should be positive.

From referring Row $S^2 \Rightarrow \frac{18-2 K_p}{4}>0$

We have $$ 18-2 K_p>0 $$

or, $$ 18>2 K_p $$

$$ \text { or } K_P<9 $$

Hence, $K_P$ Ranges from $-1

Now referring the Row Row $S^{\prime}$

We get

$$ \frac{32 K_i+\left(2 K_p-18\right)\left(2 K_p+2\right)}{2 K_p-18}>0 $$

or,  $$ 32 K_i+\left(2 K_p-18\right)\left(2 K_p+2\right)>0 $$

or,  $$ 32 K_i>36+32 K_p-4 K_P^2 $$

$$ \therefore \quad K_i>\frac{36+32 K_p-4 K_P^2}{32} $$

Range of $K_i$

List from  $$ 0 < K_i < \frac{36+32 K_p-4 K_P^2}{32} $$

If we have  $$ K_P=-1 \Rightarrow K_I=0 $$

If we have  $$ K_P=9 \Rightarrow K_I=0 $$

To determine the Max value of $K_I$ in the equation we have to determine

$$ \frac{d K_I}{d K_p}=0 $$

$$ \begin{array}{r}or, 32-8 K_p=0 \\ \Rightarrow K_P=4 \end{array} $$

or, When we have $K_P=4$, we get

$$ \begin{aligned} K_I & =\frac{36+32 \times 4-64}{32} \\ & =3.125 \end{aligned} $$

$$ \therefore K_{I_{\max }}=3.125 $$

Hence, the correct answer is (3.1 to 3.2).

content:
The loop transfer function of a negative feedback system is

$$ G(s) H(s)=\frac{K(s+11)}{s(s+2)(s+8)} $$

The value of $K$, for which system is marginally stable, is $\_\_\_\_$ .
explanation:
Given : For a negative feedback system

$$ G(s) H(s)=\frac{K(s+11)}{s(s+2)(s+8)} $$

Characteristic equation is given as,

$$ \begin{aligned} & 1+G(s) H(s)=0 \\ & s\left[s^2+10 s+16\right]+K s+11 K=0 \\ & s^3+10 s^2+(16+K) s+11 K=0 \end{aligned} $$

Method 1

Preparing RH table,

$$ \begin{array}{c|ccc} s^3 & 1 & 16+K & 0 \\ s^2 & 10 & 11 K & 0 \\ s^1 & \frac{(160+10 K)-11 K}{10} & 0 & 0 \\ s^0 & 11 K & 0 & 0 \end{array} $$

At $K=K_{\text {marginal }}$, RH table should have row of zeros other then last row.

$$ \begin{aligned} So,& \frac{\left(160+10 K_{\text {marginal }}\right)-11 K_{\text {marginal }}}{10}=0 \\ & 160-K_{\text {marginal }}=0 \end{aligned} $$

$$ \therefore \quad K_{\text {marginal }}=160 $$

Method 2

From characteristic equation,

$$ s^3+10 s^2+\left(16+K_{\text {marginal }}\right) s+11 K_{\text {marginal }}=0 $$

At $K=K_{\text {marginal }}$,

$$ \begin{array}{ll} & \text { Inner product = Outer product } \\ & 1 \times 11 K_{\text {marginal }}=10\left(16+K_{\text {marginal }}\right) \\ & 11 K_{\text {marginal }}-10 K_{\text {marginal }}=160 \\ \therefore \quad & K_{\text {marginal }}=160 \end{array} $$

Consider p(s) = s<sup>3</sup> + $${a_2}$$s<sup>2</sup> + $${a_1}$$s + $${a_0}$$  with all real coefficients. It is known that its
derivative p'(s) has no real roots. The number of real roots of p(s) is

The Nyquist stability criterion and the Routh criterion both are powerful analysis tools for
determining the stability of feedback controllers. Identify which of the following statements
is FALSE.

options.content:
The closed-loop frequency response for a unity feedback system cannot be obtained
from the Nyquist plot.

The Routh criterion is not applicable in the condition of transport lag, which can be
readily handled by the Nyquist criterion.

The general shape of the Nyquist plot is readily obtained from the Bode magnitude plot
for all minimum-phase systems.
Both the criteria provide information relative to the stable gain range of the system.

content:
Match the inferences X, Y, and Z, about a system, to the corresponding properties of the elements of first column in Routh's Table of the system characteristic equation.

X: The system is stable …
Y: The system is unstable …
Z: The test breaks down …

P: … when all elements are positive
Q: … when any one element is zero
R: … when there is a change in sign of coefficients

A unity negative feedback system has the open-loop transfer function
$$$G\left(s\right)\;=\;\frac K{s\left(s\;+\;1\right)\left(s\;+\;3\right)}$$$
The value of the gain K (>0) at which the root locus crosses the imaginary axis is _________.

The forward path transfer function of a unity negative feedback system is given by 
$$$G\left(s\right)\;=\;\frac k{\left(s\;+\;2\right)\left(s\;-\;1\right)}$$$
The value of K which will place both the poles of the closed-loop system at the same
location, is ______.

Which one of the following statements is NOT TRUE for a continuous time causal
and stable LTI system?

options.content: All the poles of the system must lie on the left side of the jω axis
Zeros of the system can lie anywhere in the s-plane
All the poles must lie within $$\left|s\right|\;=\;1$$

All the roots of the characteristic equation must be located on the left side of
 the jω axis

content: A polynomial $$f\left(x\right)\;=\;a_4x^4\;+\;a_3x^3\;+\;a_2x^2\;+\;a_1x\;-\;a_0$$ with all coefficients positive has

options.content: at least one positive and one negative real root

content:
An amplifier with resistive negative feedback has two left half-plane poles in its
open-loop transfer function. The amplifier

options.content: will always be unstable at high frequencies
will be stable for all frequencies
may be unstable, depending on the feedback factor

$$$H\left(s\right)=\frac1{s^3+\alpha s^2+ks+3}$$$ is stable. The constraints on $$\alpha$$ and k are,
The open loop transfer function of a unity feedback open-loop system is $$\frac{2s^2+6s+5}{\left(s+1\right)^2\left(s+2\right)}$$. The characteristic equation of the closed loop system is
The number of roots of $$s^3\;+\;5s^2\;+\;7s\;+\;3\;=\;0$$ in the left half of the s-plane are

Consider the polynomial $p(s)=s^5+7 s^4+3 s^3-33 s^2+2 s-40$. Let $(L, I, R)$ be defined as follows.
$L$ is the number of roots of $p(s)$ with negative real parts.
$I$ is the number of roots of $p(s)$ that are purely imaginary.
$R$ is the number of roots of $p(s)$ with positive real parts.
Which one of the following options is correct?
explanation:
Given

$$ p(s)=s^5+7 s^4+3 s^3-33 s^2+2 s-40 $$

By Routh's Hurwitz criteria

$$ \begin{aligned} &\begin{array}{lcccc} & s^5 & 1 & 3 & 2 \\ & s^4 & 7 & -33 & -40 \\ & s^3 & \frac{54}{7} & \frac{54}{7} & \\ \rightarrow & s^2 & -40 & -40 & \\ & s^1 & O(-80) & 0 & \\ & s^0 & -40 & & \end{array}\\ &\text { Auxiliary equation is }\\ &\begin{aligned} A(s) & =-40 s^2-40 \\ \frac{d A(s)}{d s} & =-80 s \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { From the above table we get }\\ &L=2, I=2 \text { and } R=1 \end{aligned} $$

content:
Consider an even polynomial p(s) given by

$$p(s) = {s^4} + 5{s^2} + 4 + K$$

where K is an unknown real parameter. The complete range of K for which p(s) has all its roots on the imaginary axis is __________.

Which one of the following options correctly describes the locations of the roots of the
equation s<sup>4</sup> + s<sup>2</sup> + 1 = 0
on the complex plane?

options.content: One right half plane (RHP) root, one LHP root and two roots on the imaginary axis
Two RHP roots and two LHP roots
All four roots are on the imaginary axis

Consider a characteristic equation given by
s4 + 3s3 + 5s2 + 6s + K + 10 = 0.
The condition for stability is

Consider the feedback control system shown in figure.
(a) Find the transfer function of the system and its characteristic equation.

(b) Use the Routh-Hurwitz criterion to determine the range of 'K' for which the system is stable.
answer:
(a) Transfer function = $$\frac{K\left(s+1\right)\left(s+0.1\right)}{s^3+3.1s^2+s\left(K+0.3\right)+K}$$
Characteristic equation = $$s^3+3.1s^2+s\left(K+0.3\right)+K=0$$
(b) For K > 0 system is stable.

content:
The loop transfer function of a feedback control system is given by $$$G\left(s\right)H\left(s\right)=\frac{K\left(s+1\right)}{s\left(1+Ts\right)\left(1+2s\right)},\;K>0$$$ Using Routh-Hurwitz criterion, determine the region of K-T plane in which the
closed-loop system is stable.
answer: In the graph where K > 1 and T > 2 that is the region of stability.

content:
The characteristic equation of a feedback control system is $$$s^4+20s^3+15s^2+2s+K\;=\;0$$$
(i) Determine the range of K for the system to be stable.
(ii) Can the system be marginally stable? If so, find the required value of K and the frequency of sustained oscillation.
answer:
(i) 0 < K < 1.49
(ii) When K = 1.49 then system is marginally stable. And the frequency of sustained oscillation = $$\frac1{\sqrt{10}}rad/sec$$.

content:
A system having an open loop transfer function
$$G\left(s\right)=\frac{K\left(s+3\right)}{s\left(s^2+2s+2\right)}$$
is used in a
control system with unity negative feedback. Using the Routh-Hurwitz criterion,
find the range of values of 'K' for which the feedback system is stable.
answer: The range of K for stability: 0 < K < 4
